slides.title: AI copilots for sales teams
slides.subtitle: How revenue teams scale quality without scaling friction
slides.body: A lightweight operating model for improving seller productivity, pipeline quality, and coaching consistency.
slides.speaker_notes: Open with the business framing: this is about scalable sales quality, not generic AI hype.

slides.title: Agenda
slides.subtitle: How the discussion will move
slides.body: This deck moves from pressure and timing into benefits, operating model, and executive takeaway.
slides.speaker_notes: Use this slide to frame the flow before entering the main argument.

slides.title: Why now
slides.subtitle: The economics finally support a focused deployment model
slides.speaker_notes: Transition from headline narrative into the market timing and operational pressure on commercial teams.

slides.title: Commercial teams are under pressure to do more with the same headcount
slides.subtitle: The productivity gap is increasingly a process problem
slides.body: Most revenue teams have strong talent, but execution quality still varies across discovery, follow-up, account research, and coaching loops.
bullets: Sellers lose time re-writing emails, notes, and summaries that should be system-assisted.
Managers struggle to scale coaching because deal reviews are inconsistent and manual.
Enablement content exists, but it is not activated in the exact moment of need.
Leadership wants measurable lift without introducing workflow clutter.
slides.speaker_notes: Emphasize that the pain is operational inconsistency. Copilots are useful when embedded into specific moments of work.

slides.title: Three executive benefits
slides.subtitle: A focused copilot model creates value at multiple layers

cards.title: Higher seller leverage
cards.body: Reduce low-value drafting and research work so sellers can spend more time on customer-facing activity.

cards.title: Stronger deal consistency
cards.body: Standardize discovery summaries, follow-ups, next-step framing, and account preparation across the team.

cards.title: Better coaching signals
cards.body: Turn rep activity into clearer manager visibility so coaching focuses on decisions, not note cleanup.

slides.speaker_notes: Frame these three outcomes as the minimum viable executive case for adoption.

slides.title: Illustrative KPI impact
slides.subtitle: Example targets after a well-scoped deployment

metrics.label: seller time recovered
metrics.detail: From faster prep, recap, and follow-up drafting

metrics.label: meeting-to-opportunity conversion
metrics.detail: Driven by stronger discovery quality and cleaner next steps

metrics.label: manager coaching coverage
metrics.detail: More reps reviewed with less admin effort

slides.speaker_notes: Clarify that the numbers are illustrative targets to discuss expected shape of impact rather than a promised benchmark.

slides.title: What the operating model looks like
slides.subtitle: Keep the workflow narrow, disciplined, and measurable
slides.body: Start with a small set of high-value moments: account prep, call recap, follow-up drafting, and manager review notes.
bullets: Use structured inputs and outputs so quality can be audited.
Instrument the workflow with lightweight usage and outcome metrics.
Treat the copilot as an assistant to a process, not a replacement for judgment.
slides.speaker_notes: This slide should make the rollout feel contained and professional. The missing image is intentionally handled with a clean placeholder.

slides.title: Executive FAQ
slides.subtitle: Questions leaders usually ask before scaling rollout

faq_items.title: Where should deployment start?
faq_items.body: Start with a narrow workflow that already has clear friction, clear ownership, and measurable outcomes.

faq_items.title: How should success be measured?
faq_items.body: Measure a mix of workflow adoption, time recovered, and execution quality improvements.

faq_items.title: What is the main implementation risk?
faq_items.body: Trying to broaden scope too early before the process and quality controls are stable.

slides.speaker_notes: Use this slide to pre-empt common objections and show operational discipline.

slides.title: Closing thought
slides.quote: The winning AI sales copilot is not the loudest one — it is the one that quietly improves the quality of execution every day.
slides.speaker_notes: Close on discipline and clarity: consistent execution compounds.

slides.title: Executive summary
slides.body: The most effective deployment is narrow, process-bound, and measured against real workflow outcomes rather than generic AI enthusiasm.
bullets: Target a few high-value seller moments
Measure productivity lift before expanding scope
slides.speaker_notes: Reinforce the three most important recommendations in one place.
